task_completion.description: Screaming Frog SEO Spider application is opened.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Screaming Frog SEO Spider GUI is visible on the screen.

task_completion.description: The target URL is entered into the crawler.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The URL 'https://crawler-test.com/' is typed into the main URL input bar at the top of the Screaming Frog interface.

task_completion.description: The crawl process has been initiated.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Start' button next to the URL bar has been clicked, and the UI indicates an active or completed crawl (e.g., 'Start' changes to 'Clear' or 'Pause', or a progress bar is visible).

task_completion.description: Crawl results are populating in the main window.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The main spreadsheet-like pane in Screaming Frog shows a list of discovered URLs from crawler-test.com.

task_completion.description: At least 10 URLs have been discovered.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The main results pane lists 10 or more distinct URLs, or the 'Total URIs'/'Crawled' counter in the interface shows a number greater than or equal to 10.

integrity.description: The agent used the Screaming Frog GUI rather than a terminal or script.
integrity.visual_evidence: The screenshots show interaction with the actual Screaming Frog application window, not a command-line interface or python script.

integrity.description: The crawl results match the target website.
integrity.visual_evidence: The URLs listed in the results pane actually belong to the 'crawler-test.com' domain (e.g., paths like /mobile/, /links/, /content/). They should not be generic or from a different site.

integrity.description: The agent did not spoof the results in a text editor.
integrity.visual_evidence: The results are displayed within the Screaming Frog data grid, not typed into Notepad, Word, or a similar text editing application.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The target URL is 'https://crawler-test.com/'. A genuine crawl of this site will easily yield over 400 URLs. Look for specific paths in the results such as '/mobile/', '/links/', '/content/', and '/javascript/' to verify the crawl is authentic.
